untranslatable_reason: This signal describes a multi-leg options strategy (short straddle with staged buyback management) that requires real-time tracking of separate put and call positions, directional reversals within a straddle framework, and explicit option premium levels (3 points, 1/2 point, 1/4 point, 2 1/2 points). The backtest engine does not support: (1) multi-leg options position management, (2) individual leg pricing tracking, (3) time-decay and volatility-based exit targets for options, (4) staged partial closure logic based on option premiums. This is an options-specific tactical rule that cannot be meaningfully translated into price/technical indicator conditions.
translation_notes: Signal MCMILLAN_DRY_150 is inherently untranslatable to a price-based backtest engine. It requires options-chain data, Greeks tracking (delta/gamma/vega), and premium-based exit logic. A simplified NEUTRAL-direction proxy was partially structured (directional reversal detection via ATR and moving average crossover) but this fundamentally misrepresents the straddle buyback mechanics and the 3-point premium target constraint.
